# Original LEZAC.EXE game-over fallback (DS:79B9) runtime reachability evidence,
# captured in-container via DOSBox /proc/mem tick-locked sampling (frame counter
# DS:0x78C2). Level 1, single player: lives forced to 1 (DS:0x79EA), then the
# player was killed by standing on its own bombs. When the final life is lost
# the game runs the fallback path 1000:7ef8..7f2a (distinct from the normal
# return-to-active restore at 1000:7e85..7ea7): DS:0x79EA lives goes 1->0 and
# the DS:0x79B9 counter increments (0 -> 1, then climbs to 0x11 while the
# game-over state is held). This demonstrates the fallback is reachable at
# runtime, resolving the reachability open item.
ds79b9_fallback_original=level1
source=LEZAC.EXE via DOSBox /proc/mem runtime read
runtime_ds=0c8f
frame_counter_offset=0x78c2
fallback_path=1000:7ef8..7f2a
normal_return_path=1000:7e85..7ea7
active_gate_offset=0x79b8
counter_offset=0x79b9
lives_offset=0x79ea
energy_offset=0x79ec
before_active=1
before_counter=0
before_lives=1
death_tick=1033
after_active=1
after_counter=1
after_counter_peak=17
after_lives=0
original_reachability=1
visual_claim=0
temp_copy=1
